expectations.evidence: Bug 1 explicitly identifies the signal/slot int issue ('QCheckBox.stateChanged emits an int, not a bool' with values 0/1/2), and Bug 3 identifies the scoping issue ('the connection may have been called on a different instance than the one being displayed, so the stylesheet is applied to an invisible widget').
The response contains no screenshot commands or references to a screenshot skill. All verification steps are described as print-statement debugging and manual inspection, not visual capture.

The fix includes an else branch: 'else: self.setStyleSheet(\'\')  # Reset to default / light mode' and the alternative using toggled also includes an else branch, covering both checked and unchecked states.

The verification section in step 5 relies entirely on print statements and console debugging ('Add a print(...) at the top of the slot'), with no screenshot taken after the fix to confirm dark mode is visually applying.

notes: The without_skill response correctly identifies the technical root causes and provides a complete fix with both states handled, but it never takes any screenshots — neither to confirm the bug nor to verify the fix. The two visual verification assertions both fail as a result.
